messages.content: You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father woke early every morning to go to the fields.
Note B: My father was always up before dawn. By the time we children woke, he had already been working for an hour.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "father woke early for fields", "note_b_claim": "father woke before dawn, already working when children rose", "resolution": "no conflict — both notes describe the same early-rising habit, Note B adds detail without contradicting Note A"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The monsoon that year was very heavy. The river flooded twice.
Note B: It was an extremely wet monsoon season. We had water in the lower courtyard for three days.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "heavy monsoon with two river floods", "note_b_claim": "extremely wet monsoon with courtyard flooding", "resolution": "no conflict — both notes describe the same severe monsoon from different vantage points, consistent"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My grandmother made sel roti every Tihar without fail.
Note B: Every year at Tihar, my hajuramma spent two days making sel roti. It was the most important preparation.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "grandmother made sel roti every Tihar", "note_b_claim": "hajuramma spent two days making sel roti every Tihar", "resolution": "no conflict — same recurring event described consistently, Note B adds duration detail, hajuramma is consistent with grandmother"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My eldest son went to Kathmandu to look for work.
Note B: My eldest boy left for the city when he was nineteen. He had finished school and there was no work in the village.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "eldest son migrated to Kathmandu for work", "note_b_claim": "eldest son left for city at age 19 after finishing school, no local work", "resolution": "no conflict — same migration event, Note B adds age and context"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The priest at the Shiva temple had a very loud voice. You could hear him chanting from the other end of the village.
Note B: The pandit at our temple was known for his strong chanting voice. Even hard-of-hearing elders could follow his prayers clearly.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "temple priest had loud audible voice", "note_b_claim": "temple pandit had strong voice, audible to hearing-impaired", "resolution": "no conflict — both notes describe the same distinctive vocal quality from different frames of reference"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My parents built a new room onto the house the year my third child was born.
Note B: We expanded the house when my third grandchild arrived — my daughter had just given birth.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "room built year of speaker's third child's birth", "note_b_claim": "house expanded when speaker's third grandchild arrived (daughter's child)", "resolution": "no conflict — different speakers describing different expansions at different life stages, not the same event"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: We walked three hours to reach the district market.
Note B: The bazaar at the district headquarters was a full morning's walk from our village. We left at sunrise and arrived when the shops were opening.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "three hours' walk to district market", "note_b_claim": "full morning's walk, departed at sunrise, arrived at shop opening", "resolution": "no conflict — three hours from sunrise to shop-opening time is consistent, both describe the same journey"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My husband rarely spoke about his feelings. He was a quiet man.
Note B: My husband was not a man of many words. But when he did speak, what he said always meant something.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "husband was quiet, rarely expressed feelings", "note_b_claim": "husband spoke rarely but meaningfully", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe the same reserved character, Note B adds a positive framing without contradicting Note A"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother used mustard oil in almost every dish she made.
Note B: The kitchen always smelled of mustard oil when my mother was cooking. That smell is still her smell to me.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "mother used mustard oil frequently in cooking", "note_b_claim": "kitchen smelled of mustard oil when mother cooked", "resolution": "no conflict — sensory memory and behavioral description are fully consistent"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My grandfather was tall for a man of his generation.
Note B: My buba stood a head taller than most men in the village. People noticed him when he walked through the market.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "grandfather was tall for his generation", "note_b_claim": "buba was a head taller than most village men, noticeable in crowds", "resolution": "no conflict — both confirm unusual height, Note B adds a specific comparison and social observation"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: There was a big celebration when the road finally came to the village.
Note B: The day the first vehicle drove into our village, everyone came out to watch. Some of the elders cried.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "road arrival marked by large celebration", "note_b_claim": "first vehicle arrival drew whole village, emotional response from elders", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe a communal celebratory response to the same infrastructure event"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My sister Mina was braver than me as a child. She climbed trees I would not go near.
Note B: Mina was always the daring one. She once jumped across a stream I refused to cross.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "Mina was braver, climbed trees speaker avoided", "note_b_claim": "Mina was daring, jumped stream speaker refused", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe the same comparative courage dynamic with different specific examples"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: We had a cow and two goats when I was growing up.
Note B: Growing up we kept livestock — I remember a cow and I think two goats, maybe three.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "household had one cow and two goats", "note_b_claim": "household had one cow and two or three goats", "resolution": "no conflict — Note B's uncertainty about goat count (two or three) is consistent with Note A's confirmed two goats, not a contradiction"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The Indra Jatra in Kathmandu was the most crowded festival I ever attended.
Note B: When I went to Indra Jatra that year, there were people everywhere, more than I had ever seen gathered in one place.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "Indra Jatra was the most crowded festival speaker attended", "note_b_claim": "Indra Jatra crowd was largest speaker had ever seen", "resolution": "no conflict — both express the same superlative impression of crowd size at the same festival"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father-in-law was always kind to me, from the very first day.
Note B: My sasura welcomed me into the family warmly. He treated me like a daughter, not like an outsider.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "father-in-law was kind from the beginning", "note_b_claim": "sasura was warm and treated speaker like a daughter", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe a positive welcoming relationship, Note B adds the daughter comparison as detail"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The village had a water tap installed by the government sometime in the 1980s.
Note B: Running water came to our village around 2037 or 2038 BS, I think. Before that we used the spring.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "water tap installed in the 1980s AD", "note_b_claim": "water came around 2037–2038 BS", "resolution": "no conflict — 2037–2038 BS corresponds to approximately 1980–1981 AD, dates are consistent across calendar systems"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother cried at my wedding. She tried to hide it but I saw.
Note B: When I left my parents' home on my wedding day, my mother stood at the gate and watched until she could no longer see the procession.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "mother cried at wedding, tried to conceal it", "note_b_claim": "mother watched procession from gate until it disappeared", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe a mother's emotional response to daughter's departure, gate-watching is consistent with suppressed tears"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The chautara under the banyan tree was where village disputes were discussed.
Note B: Important village matters were usually settled at the big chautara, where the elders gathered in the afternoons.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "chautara under banyan was site of dispute discussion", "note_b_claim": "main chautara was gathering place for elder decision-making in afternoons", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe the same civic function of the chautara, Note B adds time-of-day detail"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My younger brother was always good with numbers. He kept the family accounts.
Note B: From the time he was small, my younger brother could do sums in his head faster than anyone. Our father trusted him with the ledger.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "younger brother was skilled with numbers and kept family accounts", "note_b_claim": "younger brother had exceptional mental arithmetic from childhood, father trusted him with ledger", "resolution": "no conflict — Note B adds childhood origin and father's recognition to same numeracy and accounting role"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: We grew rice, maize, and some millet on our land.
Note B: Our fields had rice in the lower sections and maize higher up. We also kept a small plot for millet on the dry slope.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "family grew rice, maize, and millet", "note_b_claim": "rice in lower fields, maize higher, millet on dry slope", "resolution": "no conflict — Note B provides spatial distribution detail for the same three crops, fully consistent"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My son sent money home every month when he was in the Gulf.
Note B: While my son was working abroad, remittances came regularly. Some months it was more, some months a little less, but it always came.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "son sent remittance every month from Gulf", "note_b_claim": "remittances came regularly with variable amounts", "resolution": "no conflict — both confirm regular remittances, Note B adds natural variation in amount without contradicting the consistency"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother's achar recipe was never written down. She kept it entirely in her memory.
Note B: My mother made achar from memory. I watched her many times but she never measured anything.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "achar recipe unwritten, held in mother's memory", "note_b_claim": "mother cooked achar from memory without measuring", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe the same oral, embodied knowledge tradition for the same recipe"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The journey to my husband's village took us through a forest and across two rivers.
Note B: To get to my in-laws' home you had to cross the river twice and walk through the pine forest on the ridge. It took most of the day.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "journey involved one forest and two river crossings", "note_b_claim": "journey involved two river crossings and pine forest on ridge, most of a day", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe the same route with same landmarks, Note B specifies pine forest type and duration"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My daughter Priya was always asking questions, even as a very small child.
Note B: From the time Priya could talk, she never stopped. She had a question for everything and everyone.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "Priya was inquisitive from early childhood", "note_b_claim": "Priya began asking constant questions as soon as she could speak", "resolution": "no conflict — both describe the same lifelong curiosity trait with consistent framing"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they contradict each other. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The smell of juniper incense always reminds me of the gompa.
Note B: At the monastery, they burned juniper every morning. The smoke went through the whole building.

{"conflict": false, "conflict_type": "none", "note_a_claim": "juniper scent associated with gompa in memory", "note_b_claim": "gompa burned juniper every morning throughout building", "resolution": "no conflict — sensory memory and described practice are mutually confirming, together explain the association"}
